description:
A technical writer is reorganizing some API reference material that lives in
    your home directory.  
    The starting file is:

        /home/user/docs/source_documentation.csv

    Content of that file (4 lines, including header):

        endpoint,method,description,author
        /users,GET,Retrieve all users,alice
        /users,POST,Create a user,bob
        /orders,GET,Retrieve all orders,carol

    Goal
    ----
    1. Use standard GNU command–line tools that manipulate columns
       (for example, cut and/or paste) to create a **new** tab-separated file

           /home/user/docs/endpoint_method.tsv

       with the following exact contents (4 lines, including header):

           method	endpoint
           GET	/users
           POST	/users
           GET	/orders

       •  Column order is “method” first, then “endpoint”.  
       •  Columns must use a single **tab (0x09)** as the delimiter, no spaces.  
       •  A trailing newline is required at the end of the last line.

    2. After creating the .tsv file, write a small one-line log to

           /home/user/docs/creation.log

       The log must contain only the line-count of the new file, formatted
       exactly like this (including the trailing period):

           Lines written: 4.

       The automated tests will check both the .tsv file’s exact byte-for-byte
       content and the single line in creation.log.

    Constraints
    -----------
    •  Solve the task from the command line in as few steps as you reasonably can
       (2–3 commands is sufficient).  
    •  Do not modify the original CSV file.  
    •  Do not hard-code the answer with echo; derive it from the CSV using
       column-manipulation utilities.

    What to deliver
    ---------------
    By the end, the file system should contain:

        /home/user/docs/endpoint_method.tsv   (with the exact TSV content above)
        /home/user/docs/creation.log          (containing: “Lines written: 4.”)

    Nothing else needs to be printed or saved.
truth:
The file /home/user/docs/source_documentation.csv exists before the agent
    starts and contains exactly (including trailing newline):

endpoint,method,description,author
/users,GET,Retrieve all users,alice
/users,POST,Create a user,bob
/orders,GET,Retrieve all orders,carol

    After successful completion, the agent must have produced:

1. /home/user/docs/endpoint_method.tsv  (byte-for-byte)

method\tendpoint\n
GET\t/users\n
POST\t/users\n
GET\t/orders\n

    That is: 4 lines, each line terminated with \n, and TAB (\x09) separating
    the two columns on each line.

2. /home/user/docs/creation.log (byte-for-byte)

Lines written: 4.\n

    No additional files should be altered or created.
